backlog.title: Bug Fixes for `#@file` Extension QA Findings
backlog.description: Remediate the four issues surfaced by creative end-to-end QA against the `#@file` extension PRD. One Major data-integrity defect (Issue 1: resolveImportPath truncation silently injects the wrong file) and three Minor issues (Issue 2: FileDetail.body byte duplication violating §12.22; Issue 3: paged expanded-view omits the paging directive per §6.3; Issue 4: image magic-number sniff deviation from §5.2, accepted as the intentional README contract — no code change). All findings are grounded in live reproduction via jiti (the same loader the test suite uses) and recorded in plan/008_561ef016260d/bugfix/001_a4cee0dcf39c/architecture/. Baseline before work: 145 + 22 + 38 = 205 shipped test cases green; `npm test` runs only file-injector.test.mjs.

milestones.title: Fix Major Issue 1: resolveImportPath truncation injects the WRONG file
milestones.description: Eliminate the silent wrong-file injection. The `extCut` heuristic at file-injector.ts:150-152 truncates a token at its last `.md`/`.markdown` substring and, when the full token is missing, resolves the truncated base `.md` instead — turning a reference to a missing `report.md.backup` into the existing `report.md`, stripping the `#@` trigger, and delivering the wrong content with no signal. Fix per PRD option 2: narrow truncation to a trailing run of markdown-formatting glue chars (`*`/`_`) + re-clean, preserving the GROUP-4a–4g formatting cases while making extended tokens exact-only per §4.4/§4.5. Update the endorsing test 4f and add primary-gate regression cases.

tasks.title: Narrow the extCut truncation heuristic to markdown-formatting glue only
tasks.description: Replace the `.md`-substring truncation in resolveImportPath with a trailing `*`/`_` strip + re-cleanToken retry that tests the EXACT path. This makes extended tokens (`.bak`, `.txt`, `.old`, path separators) exact-only, so a missing `X.md.bak` is left verbatim instead of silently resolving to `X.md`, while markdown-emphasis-glued references (`@b.md.*`) still resolve. The fix is localized to file-injector.ts:150-152 plus the resolveImportPath JSDoc. No signature change; the single call site (scanTokens:760) is unaffected.

subtasks.title: Implement narrowed fmtCut + re-cleanToken retry in resolveImportPath
subtasks.context_scope:
CONTRACT DEFINITION:
1. RESEARCH NOTE: Read plan/008_561ef016260d/bugfix/001_a4cee0dcf39c/architecture/issue1_resolveimportpath.md — it contains the VERIFIED sequencing table for every GROUP-4 case (4a–4g) and every false-positive pattern. The fix MUST reproduce that table exactly. Root cause: file-injector.ts:151 `const extCut = token.match(/^.*\.(?:md|markdown)/)` greedily truncates at the LAST `.md` substring; when candidate[0] (full token) misses, candidate[1] (truncated base .md) wins even for genuinely-missing `X.md.bak`.
2. INPUT: `token` (already cleanToken()'d by scanTokens:757) and `tryMdExt` (boolean) passed into resolveImportPath (file-injector.ts:139-162). cleanToken (lines 90-97) trims only TRAILING_PUNCT (line 25 = `.,;:!?\")]}>'`); it does NOT strip `*`, `` ` ``, or `_`.
3. LOGIC: Replace lines 150-152. Build candidates = [token]; compute `fmtCut = token.replace(/[*_]+$/, "")`; if `fmtCut !== token && fmtCut !== ""` push `cleanToken(fmtCut)` as candidate[1]. CRITICAL: the re-cleanToken(fmtCut) is load-bearing — without it, `my_file.md.*` → `my_file.md.` (trailing `.` remains) which does NOT stat as `my_file.md`, breaking GROUP-4d/4g. Keep the candidate loop (lines 153-160) and the `tryMdExt` extensionless-.md fallback UNCHANGED — do NOT consult path.extname of the fmtCut retry for .md appending (that is the separate, unchanged §4.5 rule 3). Verify against the node-confirmed table: 4a/4b/4c/4d/4g retry resolves; 4f `weird.md.bak` (missing) → null; FP `report.md.backup`/`X.md.txt`/`X.md.bak`/`X.md.old`/`X.md/foo` → null. Mock: none — resolveImportPath is pure (stats paths via isRegularFile against a temp fixture dir only).
4. OUTPUT: resolveImportPath returns `string | null` (unchanged signature). For a missing extended token it now returns null (caller scanTokens:761 leaves the `#@` marker verbatim). Direct unit probes: `resolveImportPath('report.md.backup', tmp, false)` === null; `resolveImportPath('report.md.backup', tmp, true)` === null.
5. DOCS: [Mode A] Update the resolveImportPath JSDoc (file-injector.ts:126-138) and the inline candidate comment (lines 144-149) to describe the NEW narrowed-formatting-strip behavior (trailing `*`/`_` + re-clean, not `.md`-substring truncation) and cite PRD §4.4/§4.5 exact-only for extended tokens. This rides WITH the work.

subtasks.title: Update import-behavior.test.mjs test 4f + add primary-gate regression cases
subtasks.context_scope:
CONTRACT DEFINITION:
1. RESEARCH NOTE: import-behavior.test.mjs test 4f (lines 208-213) currently ENDORSES the bug: it asserts `@weird.md.bak` (only weird.md exists) → injects `MD-MARKER`. That assertion encodes the PRD-violating behavior and MUST flip to the PRD-compliant outcome. Test 4e (both exist → weird.md.bak wins) stays valid and unchanged. See plan/008_561ef016260d/bugfix/001_a4cee0dcf39c/architecture/issue4_image_and_tests.md for the harness patterns (mk/run/has helpers, `process.exit(failed?1:0)`).
2. INPUT: The fixed resolveImportPath from P1.M1.T1.S1. The test helpers in import-behavior.test.mjs: `mk(dir, rel, body)`, `run(cwd, prompt, bareAt)`, `has(out, marker)`, `assert(c, m)`.
3. LOGIC: (a) Rewrite import-behavior.test.mjs test 4f (lines 208-213) to assert the PRD-compliant outcome: with `weird.md` existing and `weird.md.bak` NOT, `#@a.md` (containing `@weird.md.bak`) must NOT inject weird.md — assert `o.injected === 1` (only a.md) and `!has(o, 'MD-MARKER')` and that the literal `weird.md.bak` reference is left verbatim. (b) Add a NEW GROUP-4 case (4h) for the `.txt`/`.old` false-positive at the markdown-import path. (c) Add regression cases to the PRIMARY gate file-injector.test.mjs (the `npm test` gate) per the PRD Issue 1 note: a top-level case asserting `injectFiles('#@report.md.backup', ...)` with only `report.md` present yields `injected === 0` and leaves `#@report.md.backup` verbatim in text; and a markdown-import case (index.md containing `#@report.md.backup`) asserting the wrong file is not pulled in. Use the existing runCase(n, name, fn) + assert harness and mock ctx `{ cwd: TMPDIR, hasUI:false, isProjectTrusted:()=>true, ui:{notify:()=>{}} }`. Mock: filesystem fixtures only (temp dirs).
4. OUTPUT: All three harnesses green: `node ./file-injector.test.mjs`, `node ./import-behavior.test.mjs` (with 4f flipped + 4h added), `node ./relative-imports.test.mjs` unchanged. Exit 0 each.
5. DOCS: [Mode A] Update the GROUP-4 header comment in import-behavior.test.mjs (lines 191-197) to reflect that extended tokens are exact-only (truncation now strips only trailing markdown glue, not `.md` substrings). This rides WITH the work.

milestones.title: Fix Minor Issues 2 & 3: FileDetail byte duplication (§12.22) + paged directive display (§6.3)
milestones.description: Both issues live in the FileDetail → emitText → renderInjectedMessage data path and are coupled (same interface, same renderer expanded branch), so they are fixed together in one milestone. Issue 2: FileDetail.body duplicates ~100% of every deliverable file's bytes into persisted details, violating §12.22/§6.2 and roughly doubling session storage — fix by storing content offsets (slice from message.content) instead of the body string, keeping the BUG-1 (nested </file>) correctness guarantee. Issue 3: the paged expanded view shows only the head; the <paged: ...> directive text that §6.3 mandates is never surfaced — fix by storing the directive text in the paged detail and rendering it after the head.

tasks.title: Eliminate FileDetail.body byte duplication (§12.22) via content offsets
tasks.description: Stop embedding the full file body in each FileDetail. Add contentStart/contentLen (char offset+length into the assembled message.content) so the renderer slices the exact body from content (BUG-1 safe — slice by known length, not regex) without duplicating bytes. Keep `body?` as a deprecated fallback field for old/foreign/test entries (REND-* unit tests, §6.3 defensive rendering). Compute offsets post-hoc in the before_agent_start handler where content is assembled.

subtasks.title: FileDetail offset fields + emitText stops duplicating body + before_agent_start offset computation
subtasks.context_scope:
CONTRACT DEFINITION:
1. RESEARCH NOTE: Read plan/008_561ef016260d/bugfix/001_a4cee0dcf39c/architecture/issue2_3_filedetail_renderer.md. FileDetail (file-injector.ts:336-348) has `body?: string` storing the EXACT body (text=whole content; paged=head slice). emitText (878-927) sets body at 3 sites (lines 884, 908, 916). content = blocks.join("

") is assembled in before_agent_start (line 1213). A paged file = 2 blocks (head + directive) + 1 detail; text/image/binary = 1 block + 1 detail. formatTextFileBlock(abs, content) = `<file name="${abs}">
${content}
</file>` (deterministic header/footer lengths). BUG-1: a file whose content contains a literal `</file>` breaks the renderer's FILE_BLOCK_RE lazy capture — body was added to fix it; offsets preserve that fix because slicing by known length is exact.
2. INPUT: FileDetail interface (336-348); emitText (878-927); before_agent_start handler (1206-1220) where `blocks` and `details` are destructured from `pending` and `content: blocks.join("

")` is built.
3. LOGIC: (a) Add `contentStart?: number` and `contentLen?: number` to FileDetail (keep `body?` as deprecated fallback; add `directive?: string` field now too — it is populated by P1.M2.T2.S1 but declaring it here keeps the interface change atomic). (b) emitText: REMOVE `body: content` (line 884), `body: content` (line 908), and `body: head` (line 916) from the three detail pushes — real emission must NOT carry the body bytes. (c) In the before_agent_start handler, AFTER `content = blocks.join("

")` is built, compute offsets by walking details and blocks IN PARALLEL: maintain a running `blockStart` char cursor (block length + 2 for the "

" join). For each detail, consume blocks by kind: paged consumes 2 blocks (head at cursor, directive at cursor+headBlockLen+2) — the body is the HEAD block; text consumes 1 block; image/binary consume 1 block (no body). For body-bearing details (text, paged), set contentStart = blockStartOfHeadBlock + lengthOf(`<file name="${d.path}">
`) and contentLen = headBlock.length - headerLen - footerLen (where footerLen = `
</file>`.length). This is BUG-1-safe (derived from the full block string length, not regex). Do NOT mutate blocks. Edge: verify the join-separator accounting (2 chars between blocks) matches the cursor math against a multi-file fixture. Mock: none — uses the real injectFiles output shapes against temp fixtures.
4. OUTPUT: For a real injected file, `details[i]` carries contentStart/contentLen and does NOT carry body (hasOwnProperty('body') === false). The renderer (P1.M2.T1.S2) consumes these. The pre-existing REND-* unit tests that craft details WITH body still render via the fallback path.
5. DOCS: [Mode A] Update the FileDetail interface comment (file-injector.ts:336-348): document contentStart/contentLen as the offset-based body source (§12.22 compliant), mark `body?` as a deprecated fallback for old/test entries, and document `directive?` (paged-only). Cite PRD §12.22 and §6.2. This rides WITH the work.

subtasks.title: renderInjectedMessage consumes content offsets (keep body/regex fallback)
subtasks.context_scope:
CONTRACT DEFINITION:
1. RESEARCH NOTE: renderInjectedMessage (file-injector.ts:631-671) builds a `bodies: string[]` regex fallback from FILE_BLOCK_RE (line 609) at lines 639-644, then the expanded branch (line 665) does `const body = typeof d.body === 'string' ? d.body : bodies[i]`. Once emitText stops setting body (P1.M2.T1.S1), real files fall through to bodies[i] (regex) — which re-opens BUG-1 for nested-</file> content. This subtask closes that by preferring the offset slice.
2. INPUT: FileDetail now carries contentStart/contentLen (from P1.M2.T1.S1) for real files; message.content is the joined blocks string. The existing bodies[] regex fallback and the `d.body` fallback must be RETAINED for old/foreign/test entries (REND-* unit tests pass crafted details; §6.3 defensive rendering).
3. LOGIC: In the expanded branch (lines 664-670), change body resolution to a 3-tier preference: (1) if `d.contentStart != null && d.contentLen != null && typeof message?.content === 'string'` → `body = message.content.slice(d.contentStart, d.contentStart + d.contentLen)`; (2) else if `typeof d.body === 'string'` → body = d.body (old/test fallback); (3) else → body = bodies[i] (regex fallback, last resort). Keep the `d.kind !== 'image'` guard and the highlightCode/getLanguageFromPath rendering unchanged. Preserve the `FILE_BLOCK_RE.lastIndex = 0` reset (line 641). Mock: renderInjectedMessage is a pure function — pass a crafted `{ content, details:{files:[...]} }` message and a fake theme stub `{ bg:(k,t)=>t, fg:(k,t)=>t, bold:(t)=>t }`.
4. OUTPUT: Expanded view of a real injected file renders the FULL body via offset slice; a file whose content contains a literal `</file>` (BUG-1) renders the full body (not truncated); REND-11 (nested </file> unit test) still passes via whichever tier applies; REND-8 defensive fallback (no details) still passes.
5. DOCS: [Mode A] Update the renderer fallback comment (file-injector.ts:633-638) to document the 3-tier body resolution (offset → stored body → regex) and why offsets are BUG-1-safe. This rides WITH the work.

tasks.title: Surface paged-directive text in the expanded view (§6.3)
tasks.description: PRD §6.3 requires paged files to show their head block PLUS the paged-directive text verbatim in the expanded view. Currently the expanded branch reads only the head body and the `<paged: ...>` directive (which reaches the model via content) is never surfaced. Store the directive text in the paged FileDetail and render it after the head when expanded.

subtasks.title: Store directive in paged FileDetail + render after head in expanded branch
subtasks.context_scope:
CONTRACT DEFINITION:
1. RESEARCH NOTE: Read plan/008_561ef016260d/bugfix/001_a4cee0dcf39c/architecture/issue2_3_filedetail_renderer.md. emitText paged branch (file-injector.ts:914-916) pushes the head block AND the directive block (formatPagedDirectiveBlock, lines 305-307) but the paged detail (line 916) stores only `body: head` — the directive string is computed (line 915) but NOT stored on the detail. The directive reaches the model via content but never the expanded view. Reproduction: a paged detail has no `directive` field (hasOwnProperty('directive') === false). NOTE: the `directive?` field is declared on FileDetail by P1.M2.T1.S1; this subtask POPULATES and CONSUMES it.
2. INPUT: formatPagedDirectiveBlock(abs, len, startLine, injectedLines) returns the full `<file name="abs"><paged: ...></file>` block string (file-injector.ts:305-307). The directive's INNER text (for display) is the `<paged: N chars; head delivered M complete lines; read the rest with the read tool at offset:S, limit:2000, incrementing offset by 2000 until done>` substring.
3. LOGIC: (a) In emitText paged branch (around line 914-916): capture the directive block string into a local `const directiveBlock = formatPagedDirectiveBlock(abs, content.length, startLine, headLines)`, push it to state.blocks, and store the directive INNER text on the detail — `directive: extractDirectiveInner(directiveBlock)` where extractDirectiveInner strips the `<file name="...">` prefix and `</file>` suffix to leave the `<paged: ...>` text (a small inline helper, or slice between the first `>` after the name and the last `</file>`). (b) In renderInjectedMessage expanded branch, AFTER rendering the head body (the offset/body block added by P1.M2.T1.S2), if `d.kind === 'paged' && typeof d.directive === 'string'` add a new Text child: `box.addChild(new Text(theme.fg('dim', d.directive), 0, 0))` so the resume instructions appear under the head when expanded. Keep the collapsed readLine (lines 678-690) unchanged (it already shows d.range in warning color). Mock: renderInjectedMessage with a crafted paged detail `{ kind:'paged', path, range:':1-', directive:'<paged: ...>', contentStart, contentLen }` + content string + fake theme stub.
4. OUTPUT: Expanding (ctrl+o) a paged file's green `read <path>:<n>-` line now shows the head body FOLLOWED by the `<paged: ...>` directive text. The model still receives the directive via content (unchanged). A regression case in the primary gate asserts the expanded renderer output for a paged file contains the directive text.
5. DOCS: [Mode A] Update the emitText paged comment (file-injector.ts ~909-916) and the FileDetail `directive?` field comment to note it is rendered in the expanded view per §6.3. This rides WITH the work.

milestones.title: Test-suite hardening & changeset-level documentation sync
milestones.description: Wire the two standalone regression harnesses (import-behavior.test.mjs, relative-imports.test.mjs) into `npm test` so the truncation behavior (and the relative-imports bug classes) are gated going forward — this must land AFTER the Issue 1 test 4f fix so the newly-wired suite is green. Then perform the changeset-level documentation sweep (Mode B): update README.md to reflect the Issue 1 behavior change and record that the Issue 4 image magic-byte sniff is the intentional contract. Issue 4 itself needs no code change (the README already documents the stricter behavior; the PRD is read-only).

tasks.title: Wire all three test harnesses into npm test
tasks.description: package.json currently runs only file-injector.test.mjs under `npm test`. import-behavior.test.mjs (which encodes the truncation behavior, including the now-fixed test 4f) and relative-imports.test.mjs (BUG CLASS 1 & 2 regression guards) are standalone scripts not gated by CI. Chain all three in the test script so future regressions are caught.

subtasks.title: Update package.json test script to chain all three harnesses
subtasks.context_scope:
CONTRACT DEFINITION:
1. RESEARCH NOTE: See plan/008_561ef016260d/bugfix/001_a4cee0dcf39c/architecture/issue4_image_and_tests.md. package.json line 9 is `"test": "node ./file-injector.test.mjs"`. The other two harnesses exit `process.exit(failed ? 1 : 0)` (identical pattern), so they chain cleanly with `&&` (fail-fast on first non-zero exit). DEPENDENCY: must run AFTER P1.M1.T1.S2 so import-behavior.test.mjs (with flipped 4f + new 4h) is green — otherwise `npm test` goes red immediately. Confirm all three are green standalone before editing the script.
2. INPUT: package.json (14 lines). The three harness files at repo root.
3. LOGIC: Change package.json `scripts.test` to `"node ./file-injector.test.mjs && node ./import-behavior.test.mjs && node ./relative-imports.test.mjs"`. Do not add a test framework or new dependencies (zero-deps ethos). Preserve the `typecheck` script. Validate ordering: file-injector.test.mjs first (primary gate, most cases), then the two regression suites. Mock: none.
4. OUTPUT: `npm test` runs all three harnesses sequentially and exits 0 only if ALL pass. A regression in any of the three now fails `npm test`.
5. DOCS: [Mode A] None needed beyond package.json itself (the script is self-documenting). No README change for this subtask — the README feature/docs sweep is P1.M3.T2.S1.

tasks.title: Sync changeset-level documentation
tasks.description: Mode B final documentation sweep across the whole changeset. Update README.md to reflect (a) the Issue 1 behavior change (extended tokens like X.md.bak are now exact-only and left verbatim, not silently truncated to the base .md), and (b) explicitly note that the Issue 4 image magic-number sniff is the intentional, documented contract (the README already states images are matched by real bytes — verify and cross-reference). No PRD.md edit (read-only).

subtasks.title: Update README.md for Issue 1 behavior change + Issue 4 intentional-contract note
subtasks.context_scope:
CONTRACT DEFINITION:
1. RESEARCH NOTE: README.md is 138 lines. The image-bytes contract is at README.md:66 ("Images are matched by their real bytes, not just the extension..."). The 'What gets injected' table is at lines 44-51. There is no per-issue behavior section for token resolution. This is the FINAL changeset-level doc task (Mode B) — it summarizes the whole delta and must run AFTER every implementing subtask (it depends on all of them). Do NOT edit PRD.md (read-only, owned by humans).
2. INPUT: All implementing subtasks complete (P1.M1.* fix + tests; P1.M2.* offset/directive fixes; P1.M3.T1.S1 test wiring). The behavior changes to document: (Issue 1) `#@X.md.bak` where only `X.md` exists is now left verbatim (was silently truncated to X.md); markdown emphasis `@b.md.*` still resolves. (Issue 4) image magic-byte validation is intentional — no change, but verify the README wording is accurate and add a one-line cross-reference that a real image with a mismatched extension is treated by its actual bytes.
3. LOGIC: (a) If README has a 'Syntax' or resolution section, add/update a concise note that file references carrying an extension are exact-match: a missing `X.md.bak` is left as-is (never silently resolved to `X.md`), while markdown formatting glued to a name (`*@b.md*`) is still trimmed. If no such section exists, add a short bullet under the existing 'What gets injected' / behavior area. (b) Verify README.md:66 still accurately describes the magic-byte contract; if needed, clarify that a genuine image renamed with a wrong extension (e.g. a PNG named .jpg) is delivered by its real byte type, not the declared extension. Keep edits minimal and consistent with the existing tone. Do not describe internal offset/directive implementation details (those are code-internal, not user-facing).
4. OUTPUT: README.md accurately reflects shipped behavior with no stale claims. The Issue 1 exact-only rule and the Issue 4 magic-byte contract are both discoverable from the README.
5. DOCS: This subtask IS the changeset-level documentation (Mode B final task). No further doc subtask is created.
